title: quercus infectoria var. boissieri
intro: Quercus infectoria var. boissieri, also known as Chinese gall oak or Boissier's oak, is a variety of the Quercus infectoria tree, native to China and other parts of Asia. It is a type of tree that produces galls, which have been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to their rich content of tannins and other bioactive compounds. The medicinal properties of Quercus infectoria var. boissieri include its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which are believed to contribute to its health benefits, such as improving digestion, reducing fever, and treating skin conditions like eczema. Herbal preparations of Quercus infectoria var. boissieri include teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to treat various health conditions, and it is often used in combination with other herbs in traditional Chinese medicine.
